result.summary: Linode exposes one public developer API surface (Linode API v4) and one CLI (linode-cli); both use personal access tokens, and the API also supports OAuth client-based auth.

linode_pat.label: Personal access token
linode_pat.setup: Go to [Cloud Manager](https://cloud.linode.com/), open your profile menu, choose **API Tokens**, then **Create a Personal Access Token**. Pick a label, expiry, and the minimum access needed. The token is shown only once, so copy it immediately and store it securely. You can also create or revoke it via the Linode API.

linode_oauth_client.label: OAuth client (client ID + secret)
linode_oauth_client.setup: Create an OAuth client in [Cloud Manager](https://cloud.linode.com/) under **API Tokens** / OAuth clients, or via the Linode API. Save the `client_id` and `client_secret` when they are created; the secret is only shown once. Use a public client for apps that cannot safely keep a secret, and a private client when you can.
